sanskrit:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथा: |
येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ||
transliteration:
bhayād raṇād uparataṃ maṃsyante tvāṃ mahārathāḥ
yeṣāṃ ca tvaṃ bahumato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam
literal_meaning: Out of fear / from battle / having withdrawn / they will think / of you / the great chariot-warriors / of whom / also / you / highly esteemed / having been / you will go / into lightness (diminished worth)
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns Arjuna that if he retreats, the very warriors who once honored him will re-label his exit as cowardice, collapsing his social identity and self-esteem.
psychological_state: Anticipatory shame spiral: the ego foresees total devaluation by its reference group, converting admiration into contempt.
trigger_scenario: A respected surgeon about to operate on a high-risk case imagines colleagues branding him a coward if he steps down, so paralysis replaces prudent referral.
anchor_text: Stand your ground; retreat brands you a coward and shatters the honor others once gave.
